# sharpen — showcase

A self-contained page that shows what the `sharpen` skill does, by example and by experiment.

**Section 01 — tunings.** Four real rough prompts, each shown after `/sharpen` with a
Claude Fable 5 / Claude Opus 4.8 toggle. Red-pen marks = only in the Fable tuning;
blue-pencil marks = only in the Opus tuning. Every sharpened text is the skill's actual
output for that rough prompt.

**Section 02 — proof.** Two live A/B pairs. Both sides of each pair were generated by the
same model (Claude Fable 5), same harness, same day — the only variable was the prompt
(the exact texts from Section 01). Single representative runs, not best-of-N.

**Section 03 — process.** One representative session replay: scout → interview with
defaults → rewrite.

## Run it

```sh
cd showcase
python3 -m http.server 8000   # → http://localhost:8000
```

(`file://` works too, except the copy buttons need http/localhost for the clipboard API.)

## Structure

```
showcase/
  index.html      the page (self-contained: inline CSS/JS, no dependencies)
  experiments/    6 files: <nn>-<slug>--{rough,sharpened}.html, plus the dashboard sharpened arm history (v1, v2), loaded as iframes
```

The page dogfoods both skills: built from a sharpened prompt (it's in the footer), and
its interface follows the finesse spec (motion tokens, direction-correct easing,
`:focus-visible` rings, `prefers-reduced-motion` substitution, tabular numbers).
